articles.title: Where does your favourite Cyberabad restaurant rank? Check hygiene scores of 39 food establishments released by CMC
articles.sum: Cyberabad Municipal Corporation’s food safety teams assessed hygiene standards, food handling practices, pest control measures, storage conditions, labelling, record maintenance and compliance with FSSAI norms

articles.sum: Justice G.K. Ilanthiraiyan dismisses a criminal revision petition filed by the former Minister against the complaint having been taken cognisance of by a metropolitan magistrate

articles.title: A terrorist, is a terrorist: India calls for collective action to root out murderous ideology
articles.sum: Irrespective of any grievance, political cause or strategic calculation, terrorism in all its forms and manifestations must be condemned unequivocally, says India’s Permanent Representative to the UN Ambassador Harish Parvathaneni.

articles.title: PM Modi marching to 'environmental disaster' in Great Nicobar: Congress
articles.sum: The nation's ecological conscience is on trial, says Congress leader Jairam Ramesh while raising concerns over the Great Nicobar Project.

articles.sum: Trinamool Congress MP Mahua Moitra alleged a mob attack by BJP workers at a party office in West Bengal's Nadia district. She identified 16 individuals, claiming police remained silent spectators for four hours as protesters hurled eggs and mud. Moitra was later escorted out by p…

articles.title: Japanese PM Sanae Takaichi meets PM Modi, receives ceremonial welcome at Rashtrapati Bhawan
articles.sum: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi received a ceremonial welcome at Rashtrapati Bhavan today, marking the start of her three-day official visit to India. She will attend the 16th India-Japan Annual Summit with Prime Minister Narendra Modi. This visit is poised to strengthen t…

articles.sum: The mother of the RG Kar Medical College and Hospital rape and murder victim, BJP MLA Ratna Debnath, has declared she knows the identities of those responsible for her daughter's death and the alleged cover-up. She vowed that all culprits, including those who concealed evidence, …

articles.title: Critical minerals to semiconductor: What's on the agenda as Japan PM reaches India
articles.sum: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi is in New Delhi for a three-day visit, focusing on the 16th India-Japan Annual Summit. Discussions will center on deepening cooperation in economic security, resilient supply chains, emerging technologies, and defence. Both nations aim to bo…

articles.title: How rich must Indians be for India to be called developed?
articles.sum: India is on a trajectory to potentially achieve high-income status by the late 2040s, but requires sustained annual per capita GDP growth of at least 9%. Accelerating this growth hinges on boosting female workforce participation, enhancing capital formation through improved credi…

articles.title: Tamil Nadu govt moves SC against Madras HC order banning cow slaughter
articles.sum: Tamil Nadu has approached the Supreme Court challenging the Madras High Court's sweeping ban on cow and calf slaughter. The state argues the High Court's order, based on a 1976 ruling, contradicts the Tamil Nadu Animal Preservation Act, 1958. This Act permits the slaughter of old…

articles.title: Indian Army’s field hospital saves elderly woman rescued from rubble in quake-hit Venezuela
articles.sum: The Indian Army's Operation Amistad has successfully rescued a 79-year-old Venezuelan woman from earthquake rubble, providing critical medical care at a field hospital in Caracas. The woman, suffering from a fractured leg and severe arterial issues, received specialized treatment…

articles.title: India, Malaysia review entire spectrum of bilateral military cooperation
articles.sum: India and Malaysia have reviewed their military cooperation, noting steady progress in defense engagements. Discussions covered exchanges, exercises, training, and emerging domains. Both nations agreed to boost collaboration in defense industry, cybersecurity, and disaster relief…

articles.title: Farming worries after India records driest June in over a decade
articles.sum: Weaker monsoon rains are likely to impact farm output but the government says it has enough grain stocks to deal with any shortages.

articles.title: India's biggest share sales tell the story of a country glued to its phones
